compiled_state.author: Joe Oakhart

comments.author: Joe Oakhart
comments.body:
joe-debug investigation complete — root causes proven, two fixes shipped, rest tracked.

CORRECTION to the original report: the b043/seedy-edema-groan "open + completion-signature"
symptom is NOT missing-event corruption. b043's last event is a legitimate reconciler
STATUS closed→open (Jira-authoritative reopen); the signature persists in the snapshot's
compiled_state. There IS a reopen event (authored by "reconciler"). Separate phenomenon
from the fsck corruption.

CONFIRMED ROOT CAUSES (fishbone; flock is local-only and never covered these cross-machine paths):
- RC1 (SNAPSHOT_INCONSISTENT, ~2400): the Jira reconciler CI workflow used shallow
  (--depth=1) fetch + `git rebase origin/tickets`. The shallow graft defeats git's
  merge-base, so the rebase re-applies the reconciler's stale pre-compaction worktree
  over a laptop COMPACT commit, resurrecting the source files compaction deleted while
  keeping the SNAPSHOT. Proven by (a) production artifact — commit 51e0a578c is
  single-parent off COMPACT 3afe230a0 and re-adds 8 byte-identical deleted files
  (stale-boundary fingerprint), and (b) controlled experiment: only shallow+rebase
  reproduces; full+merge and full+rebase are clean; shallow+merge fails safe (rc=128).
  Deeper enabler: compaction hard-os.remove()s folded files (compact.py:142) instead
  of the documented `*.retired` rename (invariant I1, docs/concurrency.md:35-36,154).
- RC2a (ORPHAN_EVENT false positives, ~519): compaction folds only KNOWN_EVENT_TYPES,
  but fsck flagged any pre-snapshot event absent from source_event_uuids — so
  REVIEW_RESULT (reducer-ignored) was falsely flagged. Deterministic, single-clone.
- RC2b (ORPHAN_EVENT, genuine, ~11 remaining): a concurrent write on a stale base gets
  an HLC timestamp below a peer's snapshot horizon; a clean merge keeps it as a
  pre-snapshot event that replay silently skips — real data loss. Still surfaced.

SHIPPED NOW:
- Fix #1: reconcile-bridge.yml + reconcile-bridge-canary.yml → full history (fetch-depth: 0,
  no --depth=1) + `git merge --no-edit` (not rebase). Regression guard:
  tests/interfaces/store/test_reconcile_workflow_no_shallow_rebase.py (parametrized over both).
- Fix #3: fsck._check_snapshot exempts non-KNOWN_EVENT_TYPES from ORPHAN_EVENT (symmetric
  with compaction) while still flagging genuine KNOWN-type orphans. Tests:
  tests/interfaces/store/test_fsck_snapshot_checks.py (RED→GREEN + mutation-tested).
- End-to-end replication on the live store: ORPHAN_EVENT 530 → 11 (all remaining are
  genuine KNOWN-type -EDIT.json orphans). SNAPSHOT_INCONSISTENT still growing until fix #1
  is DEPLOYED (CI still runs the old workflow until this lands).

DEFERRED (tracked in new epic adept-hedge-stain / ace2-f784-4b19-4f22, discovered_from this bug):
- #2 implement the `*.retired` non-destructive compaction (durable class-fix for RC1),
- #4 snapshot-horizon-safe reconciliation (RC2b data loss),
- #5 remediate the already-corrupted live store (drive fsck to zero; gated on #1 deploy).

Fixes not yet committed/landed — pending direction on committing + Gerrit.

comments.author: Joe Oakhart
comments.body: Fixes #1 (reconciler workflows: full history + merge) and #3 (fsck ORPHAN exemption) LANDED on main via Gerrit change 318 (merged), commit 5a7548a. LLM-Review +1 and Verified +1 both green. Deferred RC2b + .retired compaction + store remediation remain tracked in epic ace2-f784-4b19-4f22.

comments.author: RebarBotNava
comments.body: Bug-sweep triage: RC1 (shallow-rebase SNAPSHOT_INCONSISTENT) + RC2a (fsck ORPHAN false positive) are FIXED by commit 5a7548a0e (rebar-ticket: f193; RED->GREEN + mutation-tested tests), which PREVENTS new corruption. However the completion-verifier correctly FAILS an honest close on 3 unmet criteria that were deferred to epic ace2-f784-4b19-4f22: (1) no test reproduces the actual concurrent-writers+compaction attack vector (shipped tests assert workflow CONFIG invariants + fsck unit logic, not a real interleaving); (2) flock does not cover the cross-machine merge-during-push critical section (the fix is the CI merge-not-rebase change, not a lock); (3) no store remediation / fsck --repair exists to drive the accumulated corruption count to zero. RECOMMEND: treat f193 as superseded by epic ace2 (which owns remediation + hardening + a true repro test), or keep open tracked under ace2. NOT force-closing as 'done' — that would fake a certification the residual scope doesn't meet.

comments.author: RebarBotNava
comments.body: FORCE_CLOSE: close gate(s) bypassed by user approval — no completion/signature attestation was signed. Reason: "Superseded by epic ace2-f784-4b19-4f22 (per user direction). RC1/RC2a fixed by 5a7548a0e (prevents new corruption); residual RC2b + store remediation (drive fsck to zero) + a true concurrent-writers+compaction repro test are owned by ace2. Closed unsigned by design — the residual is tracked, not certified here.". Session: 335612d4f.

compiled_state.description:
# BUG: Concurrent access to the ticket store produces corruption (inconsistent compaction snapshots + orphan events)

## Summary

Concurrent writers to the shared `tickets` branch are producing a **corrupted /
internally-inconsistent store**, which should not be possible. The store's design —
per-store `flock` serialization plus an append-only JSONL event log with
last-writer-append semantics and merge-on-push reconciliation — is supposed to make
concurrency safe: writes serialize under the lock, events only append, and divergent
clones converge on replay. Observed behavior contradicts that guarantee.

## Reproduction Steps

Observed live on 2026-07-05 while closing epic REB-601 (`9322-f84a-e047-4471`) with a
second agent session concurrently active on the same store (its commits show
`CREATE/CLAIM/FILE_IMPACT/REVIEW_RESULT/SIGNATURE/COMPACT` on ticket
`4563-d974-5166-419b`, plus `chore: sync events from rebar reconciler` and
`Merge origin/tickets (auto-reconcile during push retry)` commits).

1. Two sessions write to the same store (`tickets` branch), auto-committing + auto-pushing.
2. Session A closes `seedy-edema-groan` (`b043-9490-32ca-4548`) through the completion
   verifier: the event log records `STATUS(closed)` then `SIGNATURE(completion-verifier)`.
   A `COMPACT b043-9490-32ca-4548` event lands afterward (compaction rewrites history into a
   snapshot).
3. Read the ticket back (`rebar show b043-9490-32ca-4548` / `rebar list --parent 9322…`).
4. Run `rebar fsck`.

## Expected vs Actual

- **Expected:** Reads reflect the last committed events. `b043` replays to
  `status=closed` (its only status/signature events are the close + completion-verifier
  sign; there is **no** `REOPEN`/`STATUS(open)` event after them). `fsck` reports a clean
  (or near-clean) store. Concurrent writers converge without corruption.
- **Actual:**
  - `rebar show b043` and `rebar list` report `status: open` **while the same ticket
    still carries its `completion-verifier` signature** (a signature only produced at
    close) — an impossible pair. Its event log contains **no reopen event** after the
    close, so the `open` state is a bad **compaction snapshot**, not a real transition.
  - `rebar fsck` reports **2920 issues**, including many `SNAPSHOT_INCONSISTENT`
    (snapshot references a source UUID that still exists as a separate EDIT event) and
    `ORPHAN_EVENT` (pre-snapshot event not captured in `source_event_uuids`).
  - Reads flip-flop between invocations (same ticket read as `closed` then `open`),
    indicating nondeterministic replay against an inconsistent snapshot set.
  - Net: a `closed` epic (REB-601) with a child that reads `open` — a graph-integrity
    violation the completion gate is supposed to prevent.

## Suspected Area

The corruption clusters around **compaction/snapshot generation** and the
**merge-during-push reconciliation** path, not the base append path:

- Compaction may snapshot a stale/partial event set when events arrive (or are merged from
  `origin/tickets`) concurrently with the compaction — `source_event_uuids` then omits
  events that still exist independently (`SNAPSHOT_INCONSISTENT` / `ORPHAN_EVENT`), and the
  snapshot's materialized status can predate later events (the `open`-vs-signed
  contradiction).
- The `Merge origin/tickets (auto-reconcile during push retry)` path may interleave a
  remote compaction with local un-compacted events (or vice versa) such that replay is no
  longer deterministic across clones.
- Investigate whether `flock` actually covers the compaction + push/merge critical section
  (vs only individual event appends), and whether it holds across the read-modify-write of a
  snapshot. Note: the running binary also emits `unknown key 'verify.require_ticket_for_commit'
  ignored` — confirm the compaction code path under test is the current build, not a stale one.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Root cause identified and documented: the precise interleaving (compaction vs
      concurrent append / origin merge) that yields `SNAPSHOT_INCONSISTENT` /
      `ORPHAN_EVENT` and a snapshot status that contradicts later events.
- [ ] A regression test reproduces the corruption deterministically: concurrent
      writers + a compaction (and/or a merge-during-push) drive at least one
      `SNAPSHOT_INCONSISTENT`/`ORPHAN_EVENT` or a status/signature contradiction,
      failing before the fix.
- [ ] Fix ensures compaction + push/merge reconciliation is serialized (or otherwise
      made atomic) with event appends so a snapshot's `source_event_uuids` always
      captures every superseded event and replay is deterministic across clones.
- [ ] `flock` (or the equivalent guard) is verified to cover the full
      compaction/snapshot read-modify-write and the merge-during-push critical section.
- [ ] After the fix, replaying the store yields a single consistent status/signature per
      ticket (no `open`-with-completion-signature); `b043-9490-32ca-4548` and REB-601's
      children read consistently across repeated `show`/`list` calls.
- [ ] A remediation/repair path exists for the already-corrupted store: `fsck` on the
      current store no longer reports `SNAPSHOT_INCONSISTENT`/`ORPHAN_EVENT` (either a
      `fsck --repair`-style rebuild of snapshots from the event log, or documented manual
      steps), driving the current 2920-issue count to zero for these classes.
compiled_state.title: Concurrent access to ticket store produces corruption (inconsistent compaction snapshots + orphan events)
